basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Yūsuf — the sūra announced as “the most beautiful of stories” (aḥsan al-qaṣaṣ) — unfolds, in one hundred and eleven verses, the only great continuous narrative of the Qurʾān: the dream of the child of eleven stars and the sun and moon prostrating, the brothers’ jealousy, the well, the caravan that picks him up, the house of Egypt’s grandee, the temptation of the mistress at the shut doors and the confession: “I do not absolve my own soul, for the soul is given to commanding evil, save when my Lord shows mercy”; the prison and the interpretation of dreams, the elevation to stewardship, the return of the hungering brothers, the recognition “I am Joseph and this is my brother,” the gentleness of Jacob “I complain only to God of my grief,” the final prostration “this is the realisation of my former vision,” and the seal: “truly, in their stories is a lesson for those of understanding.” Qushayrī recognises everywhere the way of beautiful patience (ṣabr jamīl): Jacob who complains only to God, Joseph for whom the well becomes the school of withdrawal and the enclosure of solitude where beauty is hidden before God unveils it. The whole sūra speaks one law: God does not open a door of trial upon the souls of His friends without opening upon their hearts the doors of clarity.

versets.ar: الر ۚ تِلْكَ آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ الْمُبِينِ
versets.tr: alif lām rā tilka āyātu l-kitābi l-mubīni
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Rā. These are the verses of the manifest Book.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: communicating by detached, unassembled letters is the lovers' custom for veiling what is dear to them. The Qurʾān, though aimed at clarification, holds both allusion and declaration, the summary and the detailed; intelligences halt at the threshold of its secrets. The Beloved alone was singled out by intimate understanding — it is his secret, which no censor can pierce. “These”: the fulfilment of the promise We made you.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ك ت ب / ب ي ن
mots.sens: these are the verses of the manifest Book

versets.ar: إِنَّا أَنزَلْنَاهُ قُرْآنًا عَرَبِيًّا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: innā anzalnāhu qurʾānan ʿarabiyyan laʿallakum taʿqilūna
versets.trad: Indeed We have sent it down as an Arabic Qurʾān, that you might reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sending down of the Book and the sending of the Messenger confirm the bonds of love; whoever is deprived of true joining finds solace in the Messenger, and whoever remains veiled from the contemplation of the beloveds is consoled by the existence of the Book.

mots.racine: ق ر أ / ع ر ب

versets.ar: نَحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ أَحْسَنَ الْقَصَصِ بِمَا أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنَ وَإِن كُنتَ مِن قَبْلِهِ لَمِنَ الْغَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: naḥnu naquṣṣu ʿalayka aḥsana l-qaṣaṣi bi-mā awḥaynā ilayka hādhā l-qurʾāna wa-in kunta min qablihi la-mina l-ghāfilīna
versets.trad: We narrate to you, by revealing this Qurʾān to you, the most beautiful of stories, though before this you were among the heedless.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives several reasons for the title: “the most beautiful of stories” — because it is free of command and prohibition, whose hearing occupies the heart; because it mentions the beloveds; because Joseph forgives his brothers in it; because in it he turns away from the chamberlain’s wife who solicits him. And “you were before this among the heedless”: you did not reach this knowledge by your effort — it is gift, not gain; favour, not learning; from Us, not from you.

mots.def: qaṣṣa: to relate, follow the trace.

mots.racine: ح س ن / ق ص ص

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ يُوسُفُ لِأَبِيهِ يَا أَبَتِ إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ أَحَدَ عَشَرَ كَوْكَبًا وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ رَأَيْتُهُمْ لِي سَاجِدِينَ
versets.tr: idh qāla yūsufu li-abīhi yā abati innī raʾaytu aḥada ʿashara kawkaban wa-sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara raʾaytuhum lī sājidīna
versets.trad: When Joseph said to his father: “O my father! I saw eleven stars and the sun and the moon — I saw them prostrating before me.”
versets.commentaire: When Joseph relates the vision, Jacob senses its truth — hence his ceaseless remembrance during the absence. Qushayrī: the right of a secret is to be kept, even from one close to you; when Joseph disclosed the secret of his vision to his father, trial fell upon him. The dream, unlike the act, falls outside choice: it is exempt from reproach.

versets.ar: قَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تَقْصُصْ رُؤْيَاكَ عَلَىٰ إِخْوَتِكَ فَيَكِيدُوا لَكَ كَيْدًا ۖ إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ لِلْإِنسَانِ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: qāla yā bunayya lā taqṣuṣ ruʾyāka ʿalā ikhwatika fa-yakīdū laka kaydan inna sh-shayṭāna li-l-insāni ʿaduwwun mubīnun
versets.trad: He said: “O my son! Do not relate your vision to your brothers, lest they devise some scheme against you. Satan is a manifest enemy to man.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the decree comes, neither warning nor caution avails — Jacob counselled with all his might, yet what was to be was. And Joseph, by disobeying his father’s counsel in exposing his dream, was not spared trial. The disclosed secret summons the trial: law of hidden love.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِن تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ كَمَا أَتَمَّهَا عَلَىٰ أَبَوَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْحَاقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika yajtabīka rabbuka wa-yuʿallimuka min taʾwīli l-aḥādīthi wa-yutimmu niʿmatahu ʿalayka wa-ʿalā āli yaʿqūba kamā atammahā ʿalā abawayka min qablu ibrāhīma wa-isḥāqa inna rabbaka ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: “Thus your Lord will choose you, teach you the interpretation of events, and complete His favour upon you and upon the family of Jacob, as He completed it before upon your two fathers Abraham and Isaac. Your Lord is All-Knowing, Wise.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: election (ijtibāʾ) is what the creature has no share in — every good that befalls the servant without his effort or intention belongs to election. Of this election: being preserved from what the chamberlain’s wife would solicit, throwing a veil over the brothers’ fault, and the swift forgiveness: “no reproach today.”

mots.racine: أ و ل / ح د ث

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ كَانَ فِي يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِ آيَاتٌ لِّلسَّائِلِينَ
versets.tr: la-qad kāna fī yūsufa wa-ikhwatihi āyātun li-s-sāʾilīna
versets.trad: Truly, in Joseph and his brothers there are signs for those who seek.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: signs for every tried one, that he may know how to endure; and for every favoured one, that he may know how to give thanks. In their story: the manner of pardoning the slip, the shame of the hard at the day of meeting, God’s gentleness toward His friends through preservation, and that love is akin to trial.

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالُوا لَيُوسُفُ وَأَخُوهُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىٰ أَبِينَا مِنَّا وَنَحْنُ عُصْبَةٌ إِنَّ أَبَانَا لَفِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: idh qālū la-yūsufu wa-akhūhu aḥabbu ilā abīnā minnā wa-naḥnu ʿuṣbatun inna abānā la-fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: When they said: “Joseph and his [younger] brother are dearer to our father than we, though we are a band. Our father is in manifest error.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they betrayed what they were concealing of envy; to expel it from their hearts they assailed their father to the point of calling him ‘astray’. And when they envied the preference, God made them prostrate before Joseph, that they might know the envious never reigns. They sought to cast Joseph into the bottom of the well — God raised him over the throne.

versets.ar: اقْتُلُوا يُوسُفَ أَوِ اطْرَحُوهُ أَرْضًا يَخْلُ لَكُمْ وَجْهُ أَبِيكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا مِن بَعْدِهِ قَوْمًا صَالِحِينَ
versets.tr: uqtulū yūsufa awi-ṭraḥūhu arḍan yakhlu lakum wajhu abīkum wa-takūnū min baʿdihi qawman ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: “Kill Joseph, or banish him to some land, so your father’s face will be free for you, and afterwards you will be a righteous people.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: who wants the all loses the all — they wanted Jacob’s affection wholly, and God then said: “he turned away from them.” And: they hastened the forbidden and postponed repentance by procrastination; what they delayed of repentance did not efface what they hastened of sin.

mots.racine: خ ل و / و ج ه
mots.sens: your father’s face will be free for you

versets.ar: قَالَ قَائِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ لَا تَقْتُلُوا يُوسُفَ وَأَلْقُوهُ فِي غَيَابَتِ الْجُبِّ يَلْتَقِطْهُ بَعْضُ السَّيَّارَةِ إِن كُنتُمْ فَاعِلِينَ
versets.tr: qāla qāʾilun minhum lā taqtulū yūsufa wa-alqūhu fī ghayābati l-jubbi yaltaqiṭhu baʿḍu s-sayyārati in kuntum fāʿilīna
versets.trad: One of them spoke and said: “Do not kill Joseph; if you must act, cast him into the depth of the well — some caravan will pick him up.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph’s brothers, even meeting him with harshness, were held back by the tenderness of kinship. And: because the divine decree was that Joseph reach that nearness, God cast into the speaker’s heart the words ‘do not kill Joseph’. Thus: “how often have trials encircled you — and God chose for you, while you detested what was to come.”

mots.racine: غ ي ب / ج ب ب

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا أَبَانَا مَا لَكَ لَا تَأْمَنَّا عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَنَاصِحُونَ
versets.tr: qālū yā abānā mā laka lā taʾmannā ʿalā yūsufa wa-innā lahu la-nāṣiḥūna
versets.trad: They said: “O our father! Why do you not trust us with Joseph, when we surely wish him well?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the envious one’s speech is not to be heard and his promise not to be accepted — even cloaked as counsel — for he feeds honey and gives gall to drink. Strange is Jacob’s acceptance of their pretended concern, when his heart had already sensed their scheme. But when the decree comes, the insight closes.

versets.ar: أَرْسِلْهُ مَعَنَا غَدًا يَرْتَعْ وَيَلْعَبْ وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحَافِظُونَ
versets.tr: arsilhu maʿanā ghadan yartaʿ wa-yalʿab wa-innā lahu la-ḥāfiẓūna
versets.trad: Send him with us tomorrow; he will frolic and play, and we shall be his guardians.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they awakened in Jacob the hope of a moment’s joy for Joseph, and the lover prefers his beloved’s repose to his own love. But by trusting their words “we shall be his guardians,” he entrusted creatures with guarding his beloved: whoever entrusts his beloved to enemies swallows the bitterness of trial.

mots.racine: ر ت ع / ل ع ب

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنِّي لَيَحْزُنُنِي أَن تَذْهَبُوا بِهِ وَأَخَافُ أَن يَأْكُلَهُ الذِّئْبُ وَأَنتُمْ عَنْهُ غَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: qāla innī la-yaḥzununī an tadhhabū bihi wa-akhāfu an yaʾkulahu dh-dhiʾbu wa-antum ʿanhu ghāfilūna
versets.trad: He said: “It grieves me that you should take him away. I fear the wolf may devour him while you are unmindful of him.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he was tried by the matter of the wolf because he had named it — by the tradition: “nothing is set upon the son of Adam but what he fears.” And: since the mention of the wolf fell from Jacob’s tongue, it became like an instruction by which the brothers seized the pretext. Prophetic mention, where fate slips even into vocabulary.

versets.ar: قَالُوا لَئِنْ أَكَلَهُ الذِّئْبُ وَنَحْنُ عُصْبَةٌ إِنَّا إِذًا لَّخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: qālū la-in akalahu dh-dhiʾbu wa-naḥnu ʿuṣbatun innā idhan la-khāsirūna
versets.trad: They said: “If the wolf devours him while we are a band, then we are indeed losers!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the brothers found themselves confirmed in the loss they predicted: for whoever sells a brother like Joseph for such a price deserves to be told ‘he has lost his bargain’. And: when they counted their own strength saying ‘we are a band,’ they were abandoned to themselves until they did what they did.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا بِهِ وَأَجْمَعُوا أَن يَجْعَلُوهُ فِي غَيَابَتِ الْجُبِّ ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْهِ لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُم بِأَمْرِهِمْ هَٰذَا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā dhahabū bihi wa-ajmaʿū an yajʿalūhu fī ghayābati l-jubbi wa-awḥaynā ilayhi la-tunabbiʾannahum bi-amrihim hādhā wa-hum lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: When they took him and agreed to put him in the depth of the well, We revealed to him: “You shall surely inform them of this deed of theirs when they do not perceive.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the trial fell upon Joseph, We hastened to him the announcement of good news, so that the consolation of disclosure might carry him through what he had to bear of cruel trial. Such is God’s law: He does not open a door of trial upon the souls of His friends without opening upon their hearts the doors of clarity and the subtleties of intimate friendship.

versets.ar: وَجَاءُوا أَبَاهُمْ عِشَاءً يَبْكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾū abāhum ʿishāʾan yabkūna
versets.trad: They came to their father at nightfall, weeping.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: that the liar is capable of weeping is a sign of God’s abandonment of him; in the tradition: “when a man’s hypocrisy is complete, he possesses his own eye to weep at will.” Or — they wept in truth, out of remorse for what they had committed, without confessing to their father.

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا أَبَانَا إِنَّا ذَهَبْنَا نَسْتَبِقُ وَتَرَكْنَا يُوسُفَ عِندَ مَتَاعِنَا فَأَكَلَهُ الذِّئْبُ ۖ وَمَا أَنتَ بِمُؤْمِنٍ لَّنَا وَلَوْ كُنَّا صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qālū yā abānā innā dhahabnā nastabiqu wa-taraknā yūsufa ʿinda matāʿinā fa-akalahu dh-dhiʾbu wa-mā anta bi-muʾminin lanā wa-law kunnā ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: They said: “O our father! We went off racing and left Joseph by our goods, and the wolf devoured him. You will not believe us, though we speak the truth.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: a lie wrapped in the appearance of truth does not deceive the Knower’s heart. Jacob at once grasps that the matter is other than they say — his heart informed him before the details appeared. So it is that the consequences of things strike the hearts of the truthful in summary, until time unfolds the details.

versets.ar: وَجَاءُوا عَلَىٰ قَمِيصِهِ بِدَمٍ كَذِبٍ ۚ قَالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ لَكُمْ أَنفُسُكُمْ أَمْرًا ۖ فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ۖ وَاللَّهُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾū ʿalā qamīṣihi bi-damin kadhibin qāla bal sawwalat lakum anfusukum amran fa-ṣabrun jamīlun wa-llāhu l-mustaʿānu ʿalā mā taṣifūna
versets.trad: They brought his tunic stained with false blood. Their father said: “Nay, your souls have contrived an affair. Beautiful patience! And it is God whose help is sought against what you describe.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their forgery could not make Jacob believe their lie: his heart told him the matter was otherwise. And: they were punished for what they did: God made them oblivious enough to forget to tear the tunic, so that Jacob knew their fabrication. “Beautiful patience”: pain without complaint to creatures, recourse to God alone — Jacob’s device throughout the trial.

mots.racine: د م م / ك ذ ب
ص ب ر / ج م ل

versets.ar: وَجَاءَتْ سَيَّارَةٌ فَأَرْسَلُوا وَارِدَهُمْ فَأَدْلَىٰ دَلْوَهُ ۖ قَالَ يَا بُشْرَىٰ هَٰذَا غُلَامٌ ۚ وَأَسَرُّوهُ بِضَاعَةً ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾat sayyāratun fa-arsalū wāridahum fa-adlā dalwahu qāla yā bushrā hādhā ghulāmun wa-asarrūhu biḍāʿatan wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-mā yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: A caravan came and sent their water-drawer; he let down his bucket and cried: “What a windfall! Here is a youth!” They concealed him as merchandise; and God knew what they were doing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: not all who seek something receive only what they ask: sometimes one receives beyond all hope — the caravan was content with water and found Joseph. And: all that is found is not as it is thought to be: the caravan thought it had found a slave, and Joseph in truth was free. How many disturbances arise in the world whose sole purpose is the repose of one.

mots.def: wārid: one who comes to water.

versets.ar: وَشَرَوْهُ بِثَمَنٍ بَخْسٍ دَرَاهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ وَكَانُوا فِيهِ مِنَ الزَّاهِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-sharawhu bi-thamanin bakhsin darāhima maʿdūdatin wa-kānū fīhi mina z-zāhidīna
versets.trad: They sold him for a paltry price, a few counted coins, for they were not interested in him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they did not know their loss at the time — they will arrive at it at the end. And: what is amazing is not that one sells a Joseph for a paltry price; what is amazing is that one sells one’s own time, more precious than red sulphur, for a wretched bit of this world. The day they said “I am Joseph,” shame seized them: enough for the deficient is the shyness of the day of meeting.

mots.racine: ث م ن / ب خ س

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِي اشْتَرَاهُ مِن مِّصْرَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ أَكْرِمِي مَثْوَاهُ عَسَىٰ أَن يَنفَعَنَا أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُ وَلَدًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلِنُعَلِّمَهُ مِن تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَالِبٌ عَلَىٰ أَمْرِهِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhī shtarāhu min miṣra li-mraʾatihi akrimī mathwāhu ʿasā an yanfaʿanā aw nattakhidhahu waladan wa-kadhālika makkannā li-yūsufa fī l-arḍi wa-li-nuʿallimahu min taʾwīli l-aḥādīthi wa-llāhu ghālibun ʿalā amrihi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: And the one from Egypt who bought him said to his wife: “Treat him generously! Perhaps he will benefit us, or we may take him as a son.” Thus We established Joseph in the land, that We might teach him the interpretation of events. God prevails over His command, but most people know not.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: his enemies wanted him to have no merit over his brothers; God wanted him to possess the earth — and what God willed was, not what his enemies willed. They wanted Joseph at the bottom of the well; God wanted him on the throne: “God prevails over His command.”

mots.racine: ك ر م / ث و ي

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ آتَيْنَاهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā balagha ashuddahu ātaynāhu ḥukman wa-ʿilman wa-kadhālika najzī l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: When he reached maturity, We gave him wisdom and knowledge. Thus We reward those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: among the ‘wisdom’ God gave him: authority over his own self, so that he overcame his desire and refrained from what that woman solicited of him — whoever has no authority over himself has none over others. At the age when passion peaks, God gave him the discernment that held him to truth.

mots.racine: ح ك م / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَرَاوَدَتْهُ الَّتِي هُوَ فِي بَيْتِهَا عَن نَّفْسِهِ وَغَلَّقَتِ الْأَبْوَابَ وَقَالَتْ هَيْتَ لَكَ ۚ قَالَ مَعَاذَ اللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُ رَبِّي أَحْسَنَ مَثْوَايَ ۖ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-rāwadathu llatī huwa fī baytihā ʿan nafsihi wa-ghallaqati l-abwāba wa-qālat hayta laka qāla maʿādha llāhi innahu rabbī aḥsana mathwāya innahu lā yufliḥu ẓ-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: And she in whose house he was solicited him; she shut the doors and said: “Come to me!” He said: “God be my refuge! He, my Lord, has treated me well. The unjust do not prosper.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, admirable play of opposites: when she shut upon him the doors of the dwelling, God opened for him the door of preservation (ʿiṣma); what was shut after the honour of what was opened did him no harm. And he speaks in truth: “He, my Lord” points to his true Lord — He who drew me from the well, who set in the chamberlain’s heart a place for me.

mots.racine: غ ل ق / ب و ب

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ هَمَّتْ بِهِ ۖ وَهَمَّ بِهَا لَوْلَا أَن رَّأَىٰ بُرْهَانَ رَبِّهِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنَصْرِفَ عَنْهُ السُّوءَ وَالْفَحْشَاءَ ۚ إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا الْمُخْلَصِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad hammat bihi wa-hamma bihā law-lā an raʾā burhāna rabbihi kadhālika li-naṣrifa ʿanhu s-sūʾa wa-l-faḥshāʾa innahu min ʿibādinā l-mukhlaṣīna
versets.trad: She desired him, and he would have desired her had he not seen the proof of his Lord. Thus We turned away from him evil and indecency. Truly he was of Our chosen servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what is not the human being’s act — what visits him without his choice or acquisition — is lifted from him, falling outside accountability; the mere ‘leaning’ (hamm) was not a fault for him or for her. The proof was a sign God showed him. “Salvation not in release, but in averting and purification”: his salvation lay in God’s turning evil from him.

versets.ar: وَاسْتَبَقَا الْبَابَ وَقَدَّتْ قَمِيصَهُ مِن دُبُرٍ وَأَلْفَيَا سَيِّدَهَا لَدَى الْبَابِ ۚ قَالَتْ مَا جَزَاءُ مَنْ أَرَادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوءًا إِلَّا أَن يُسْجَنَ أَوْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-stabaqā l-bāba wa-qaddat qamīṣahu min duburin wa-alfayā sayyidahā ladā l-bābi qālat mā jazāʾu man arāda bi-ahlika sūʾan illā an yusjana aw ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: They both raced to the door, and she tore his tunic from behind, and they found her master at the door. She said: “What is the requital of one who sought evil for your family, except prison or a painful punishment?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the tearing of his tunic — the garment of his world — did him no harm, after the tunic of his piety was confirmed upon him. And: she did not aim to tear the tunic: she clung to it to keep him; her act recoiled upon herself; her woe came from where she awaited her ease. “At the door”: your Lord lies in wait; when the servant departs from what he is charged with, he falls into the narrow of examination.

versets.ar: قَالَ هِيَ رَاوَدَتْنِي عَن نَّفْسِي ۚ وَشَهِدَ شَاهِدٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِهَا إِن كَانَ قَمِيصُهُ قُدَّ مِن قُبُلٍ فَصَدَقَتْ وَهُوَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: qāla hiya rāwadatnī ʿan nafsī wa-shahida shāhidun min ahlihā in kāna qamīṣuhu qudda min qubulin fa-ṣadaqat wa-huwa mina l-kādhibīna
versets.trad: He said: “It is she who sought to seduce me.” And a witness from her household testified: “If his tunic was torn from the front, then she speaks the truth and he is of the liars,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph openly proclaimed the fault, for the unfaithful one has no right to protection; he did not concern himself with tearing her veil. And since he was truthful without a witness, God made the small child speak who had not reached the age of speech — when the servant is true in himself, God does not mind making the stone speak for him.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ كَانَ قَمِيصُهُ قُدَّ مِن دُبُرٍ فَكَذَبَتْ وَهُوَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in kāna qamīṣuhu qudda min duburin fa-kadhabat wa-huwa mina ṣ-ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: but if his tunic was torn from behind, then she has lied and he is of the truthful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the material evidence confirms the secret. In the end, the truth does not remain hidden; it had to be that Joseph’s innocence be manifested to all. God made the silent speak — the object, the tunic, the infant — that His servant’s purity might shine.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا رَأَىٰ قَمِيصَهُ قُدَّ مِن دُبُرٍ قَالَ إِنَّهُ مِن كَيْدِكُنَّ ۖ إِنَّ كَيْدَكُنَّ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-lammā raʾā qamīṣahu qudda min duburin qāla innahu min kaydikunna inna kaydakunna ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: When he saw his tunic torn from behind, he said: “This is of your women’s scheming; your scheme is mighty indeed!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the matter became clear and Joseph’s innocence appeared, the chamberlain said: ‘this is of your scheme.’ The verse, he adds, proves that fornication was already forbidden in their religious law.

versets.ar: يُوسُفُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هَٰذَا ۚ وَاسْتَغْفِرِي لِذَنبِكِ ۖ إِنَّكِ كُنتِ مِنَ الْخَاطِئِينَ
versets.tr: yūsufu aʿriḍ ʿan hādhā wa-staghfirī li-dhanbiki innaki kunti mina l-khāṭiʾīna
versets.trad: Joseph, turn away from this. And you [woman], ask forgiveness for your sin: truly you have been at fault.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he did not wish to tear his wife’s veil: he told Joseph ‘turn away from this speech,’ then to her ‘ask forgiveness’. And: not everyone is fit for trial, for trial is the mark of the masters of friendship; strangers are left to themselves, not from honour, but from their smallness. Joseph, in contrast, was tried by prison — precisely because he was the friend.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ نِسْوَةٌ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ امْرَأَتُ الْعَزِيزِ تُرَاوِدُ فَتَاهَا عَن نَّفْسِهِ ۖ قَدْ شَغَفَهَا حُبًّا ۖ إِنَّا لَنَرَاهَا فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-qāla niswatun fī l-madīnati mraʾatu l-ʿazīzi turāwidu fatāhā ʿan nafsihi qad shaghafahā ḥubban innā la-narāhā fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: Some women in the city said: “The chamberlain’s wife is soliciting her servant’s soul: love has pierced her heart. We see her in manifest error.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: passion does not hide, and love is never without a tongue of blame let loose against it. When her love for Joseph became apparent, the women set against her the tongue of reproach. As she surpassed them in standing, she became the readiest target of blame.

mots.racine: ش غ ف / ح ب ب

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا سَمِعَتْ بِمَكْرِهِنَّ أَرْسَلَتْ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَعْتَدَتْ لَهُنَّ مُتَّكَأً وَآتَتْ كُلَّ وَاحِدَةٍ مِّنْهُنَّ سِكِّينًا وَقَالَتِ اخْرُجْ عَلَيْهِنَّ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَأَيْنَهُ أَكْبَرْنَهُ وَقَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَّ وَقُلْنَ حَاشَ لِلَّهِ مَا هَٰذَا بَشَرًا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا مَلَكٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-lammā samiʿat bi-makrihinna arsalat ilayhinna wa-aʿtadat lahunna muttakaʾan wa-ātat kulla wāḥidatin minhunna sikkīnan wa-qālati khruj ʿalayhinna fa-lammā raʾaynahu akbarnahu wa-qaṭṭaʿna aydiyahunna wa-qulna ḥāsha li-llāhi mā hādhā basharan in hādhā illā malakun karīmun
versets.trad: When she heard of their gossip, she sent for them, prepared a banquet for them, gave each a knife, and said to him: “Come out before them!” When they saw him, they exalted him and cut their hands, saying: “God forfend! This is no mortal; this is none but a noble angel!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were altered and bewildered to the point of speaking outside discernment: ‘this is no mortal’ — yet he was; ‘this is none but a noble angel’ — yet he was not. The meeting with the beautiful abolishes measure in them. And: the sight of Joseph shook them, but not her — for the beginning trembles, but the one rooted in meaning no longer trembles.

versets.ar: قَالَتْ فَذَٰلِكُنَّ الَّذِي لُمْتُنَّنِي فِيهِ ۖ وَلَقَدْ رَاوَدتُّهُ عَن نَّفْسِهِ فَاسْتَعْصَمَ ۖ وَلَئِن لَّمْ يَفْعَلْ مَا آمُرُهُ لَيُسْجَنَنَّ وَلَيَكُونًا مِّنَ الصَّاغِرِينَ
versets.tr: qālat fa-dhālikunna lladhī lumtunnanī fīhi wa-laqad rāwadttuhu ʿan nafsihi fa-staʿṣama wa-la-in lam yafʿal mā āmuruhu la-yusjananna wa-la-yakūnan mina ṣ-ṣāghirīna
versets.trad: She said: “This is the one for whom you blamed me. I did indeed seek to seduce him, but he held himself back. Yet if he does not do what I command him, he shall be jailed and be of the abased.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: her confession shows she wished to turn upon them the deserved blame — since they themselves had cut their hands. And she took refuge in threat: Joseph preferred prison to defilement. The true lover prefers pain in God to the enjoyment of his soul.

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِي إِلَيْهِ ۖ وَإِلَّا تَصْرِفْ عَنِّي كَيْدَهُنَّ أَصْبُ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَكُن مِّنَ الْجَاهِلِينَ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi s-sijnu aḥabbu ilayya mimmā yadʿūnanī ilayhi wa-illā taṣrif ʿannī kaydahunna aṣbu ilayhinna wa-akun mina l-jāhilīna
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord! Prison is dearer to me than what they call me to. If You do not turn their scheme from me, I shall incline to them and be of the ignorant.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial accompanies the choice; had he sought wellbeing in place of what they were calling him to, perhaps he would have been spared — but having said ‘prison is dearer to me,’ he was held to his word. And: Joseph spoke from the very depth of tawḥīd when he said ‘if You do not turn their scheme from me, I shall incline to them’: he knew his salvation lay in God’s averting of the trial, not in his own effort.

versets.ar: فَاسْتَجَابَ لَهُ رَبُّهُ فَصَرَفَ عَنْهُ كَيْدَهُنَّ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: fa-stajāba lahu rabbuhu fa-ṣarafa ʿanhu kaydahunna innahu huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: So his Lord answered him and turned away from him their scheme. Truly He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when he returned to God by the sincerity of imploration, God came swiftly to his aid; none has wavered for God’s sake without His granting relief by His generosity. “He hears” the words of those who ask; “He knows” their states.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا رَأَوُا الْآيَاتِ لَيَسْجُنُنَّهُ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: thumma badā lahum min baʿdi mā raʾawu l-āyāti la-yasjununnahu ḥattā ḥīnin
versets.trad: Then it occurred to them, after they had seen the signs, to imprison him for a time.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph was imprisoned despite the clear signs of his innocence, to shield the chamberlain’s wife — God made of it the cause by which His decree would turn the kingship to Joseph. The veil of injustice is the hidden way of elevation: such is the wage of him who endures.

versets.ar: وَدَخَلَ مَعَهُ السِّجْنَ فَتَيَانِ ۖ قَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا إِنِّي أَرَانِي أَعْصِرُ خَمْرًا ۖ وَقَالَ الْآخَرُ إِنِّي أَرَانِي أَحْمِلُ فَوْقَ رَأْسِي خُبْزًا تَأْكُلُ الطَّيْرُ مِنْهُ ۖ نَبِّئْنَا بِتَأْوِيلِهِ ۖ إِنَّا نَرَاكَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-dakhala maʿahu s-sijna fatayāni qāla aḥaduhumā innī arānī aʿṣiru khamran wa-qāla l-ākharu innī arānī aḥmilu fawqa raʾsī khubzan taʾkulu ṭ-ṭayru minhu nabbiʾnā bi-taʾwīlihi innā narāka mina l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: Two young servants entered the prison with him. One of them said: “I saw myself pressing wine.” The other said: “I saw myself bearing on my head bread of which the birds were eating. Inform us of its interpretation; truly we see you as one of those who do good.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the company of the righteous, even in prison, bears its blessing in time. By the mouth of one of these companions Joseph was drawn from the dungeon — “companionship gives its blessings, even slowly.” And the testimony “we see you as one of those who do good”: the attestation of goodness is a rope by which kindness is drawn.

mots.racine: ع ص ر / خ م ر

versets.ar: قَالَ لَا يَأْتِيكُمَا طَعَامٌ تُرْزَقَانِهِ إِلَّا نَبَّأْتُكُمَا بِتَأْوِيلِهِ قَبْلَ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمَا مِمَّا عَلَّمَنِي رَبِّي ۚ إِنِّي تَرَكْتُ مِلَّةَ قَوْمٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَهُم بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: qāla lā yaʾtīkumā ṭaʿāmun turzaqānihi illā nabbaʾtukumā bi-taʾwīlihi qabla an yaʾtiyakumā dhālikumā mimmā ʿallamanī rabbī innī taraktu millata qawmin lā yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-hum bi-l-ākhirati hum kāfirūna
versets.trad: He said: “No food provided you will come but I shall inform you of its interpretation before it comes to you. This is of what my Lord has taught me. I have left the religion of a people who do not believe in God and who deny the hereafter.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: measured pondering in the answer, not haste, is the mark of the noble: Joseph promised them the response without rushing it — when one does not give cash, let him give at least a promise. And before the interpretation, he set before them the profession of tawḥīd: he could hardly stay silent once it was a matter of God and the Beloved.

mots.racine: م ل ل / ق و م

versets.ar: وَاتَّبَعْتُ مِلَّةَ آبَائِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۚ مَا كَانَ لَنَا أَن نُّشْرِكَ بِاللَّهِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِن فَضْلِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْنَا وَعَلَى النَّاسِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ttabaʿtu millata ābāʾī ibrāhīma wa-isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba mā kāna lanā an nushrika bi-llāhi min shayʾin dhālika min faḍli llāhi ʿalaynā wa-ʿalā n-nāsi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yashkurūna
versets.trad: I have followed the religion of my fathers Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. It is not for us to associate anything with God. That is of God’s favour upon us and upon mankind, but most people are not grateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: tawḥīd itself is the grace. “That is of God’s favour upon us and upon mankind”: that He has inscribed it in us, that it is offered to all as a call — but few are grateful. Joseph links his knowledge not to his genius, but to the chain of the beloveds: Abraham, Isaac, Jacob.

versets.ar: يَا صَاحِبَيِ السِّجْنِ أَأَرْبَابٌ مُّتَفَرِّقُونَ خَيْرٌ أَمِ اللَّهُ الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ
versets.tr: yā ṣāḥibayi s-sijni a-arbābun mutafarriqūna khayrun ami llāhu l-wāḥidu l-qahhāru
versets.trad: O my two fellow-prisoners! Are diverse lords better, or God, the One, the Subjugating?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph no longer kept silent once he had begun the exposition of tawḥīd and the mention of the Adored: it is said that whoever loves a thing multiplies its remembrance. The prison thus becomes a preacher’s pulpit; chains do not constrain the lover’s tongue.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ف ر ق

versets.ar: مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِهِ إِلَّا أَسْمَاءً سَمَّيْتُمُوهَا أَنتُمْ وَآبَاؤُكُم مَّا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَانٍ ۚ إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۚ أَمَرَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: mā taʿbudūna min dūnihi illā asmāʾan sammaytumūhā antum wa-ābāʾukum mā anzala llāhu bihā min sulṭānin ini l-ḥukmu illā li-llāhi amara allā taʿbudū illā iyyāhu dhālika d-dīnu l-qayyimu wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: What you worship besides Him are nothing but names you and your fathers have named, for which God has sent down no authority. The judgment is God’s alone. He has commanded that you worship none but Him. That is the upright religion, but most people know not.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the idols are nothing but bare names — that is, human conventions cloaking the void. All authority is God’s alone. Joseph, in prison, unfolds tawḥīd as the only reality; all the rest is language.

mots.racine: د ي ن / ق و م

versets.ar: يَا صَاحِبَيِ السِّجْنِ أَمَّا أَحَدُكُمَا فَيَسْقِي رَبَّهُ خَمْرًا ۖ وَأَمَّا الْآخَرُ فَيُصْلَبُ فَتَأْكُلُ الطَّيْرُ مِن رَّأْسِهِ ۚ قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ الَّذِي فِيهِ تَسْتَفْتِيَانِ
versets.tr: yā ṣāḥibayi s-sijni ammā aḥadukumā fa-yasqī rabbahu khamran wa-ammā l-ākharu fa-yuṣlabu fa-taʾkulu ṭ-ṭayru min raʾsihi quḍiya l-amru lladhī fīhi tastaftiyāni
versets.trad: O my two fellow-prisoners! As for one of you, he will give his master wine to drink; as for the other, he will be crucified and the birds will eat from his head. The matter you two consult on has been decreed.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they shared the question, the judgment, and the prison, but their ends diverged: one crucified, the other elevated and bestowed upon. Such is the mysterious division wrought by tawḥīd: above the firmament, the elevation of one; under the earth, the tomb of the other.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ لِلَّذِي ظَنَّ أَنَّهُ نَاجٍ مِّنْهُمَا اذْكُرْنِي عِندَ رَبِّكَ فَأَنسَاهُ الشَّيْطَانُ ذِكْرَ رَبِّهِ فَلَبِثَ فِي السِّجْنِ بِضْعَ سِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lilladhī ẓanna annahu nājin minhumā udhkurnī ʿinda rabbika fa-ansāhu sh-shayṭānu dhikra rabbihi fa-labitha fī s-sijni biḍʿa sinīna
versets.trad: And he said to the one of them he thought would be saved: “Mention me to your master.” But Satan made him forget the mention of his master, so Joseph remained in prison for some years.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph was reproached for forgetting — in his speech — the One whose aid he should have sought, when he said ‘mention me to your master’. In some revealed Books: “O son of Adam, teach freely as you were taught freely”. To have sought help from a creature lengthened his stay in prison — thus God repays whoever attaches his heart to another.

mots.racine: ب ض ع / س ن و

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الْمَلِكُ إِنِّي أَرَىٰ سَبْعَ بَقَرَاتٍ سِمَانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجَافٌ وَسَبْعَ سُنبُلَاتٍ خُضْرٍ وَأُخَرَ يَابِسَاتٍ ۖ يَا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَأُ أَفْتُونِي فِي رُؤْيَايَ إِن كُنتُمْ لِلرُّؤْيَا تَعْبُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla l-maliku innī arā sabʿa baqarātin simānin yaʾkuluhunna sabʿun ʿijāfun wa-sabʿa sunbulātin khuḍrin wa-ukhara yābisātin yā ayyuhā l-malaʾu aftūnī fī ruʾyāya in kuntum li-r-ruʾyā taʿburūna
versets.trad: The king said: “I saw [in a dream] seven fat cows devoured by seven lean ones, and seven green ears and others withered. O dignitaries, explain to me my vision, if you can interpret dreams.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the beginning of Joseph’s trial was a dream he made known; the cause of his deliverance was another dream the king made known; that one may know God does as He wills — by what He made his trial, by that He made his salvation.

mots.racine: س ب ع / ب ق ر / س م ن

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَضْغَاثُ أَحْلَامٍ ۖ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِتَأْوِيلِ الْأَحْلَامِ بِعَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: qālū aḍghāthu aḥlāmin wa-mā naḥnu bi-taʾwīli l-aḥlāmi bi-ʿālimīna
versets.trad: They said: “A muddle of dreams, and we are not knowing in the interpretation of dreams.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the state of the dream is not altered by the interpreter’s error — they declared it confused, and this did not harm the rightness of its meaning. Whoever seeks a thing outside its place does not obtain what he seeks.

mots.racine: ض غ ث / ح ل م

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِي نَجَا مِنْهُمَا وَادَّكَرَ بَعْدَ أُمَّةٍ أَنَا أُنَبِّئُكُم بِتَأْوِيلِهِ فَأَرْسِلُونِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhī najā minhumā wa-ddakara baʿda ummatin anā unabbiʾukum bi-taʾwīlihi fa-arsilūni
versets.trad: And the one of the two who had been saved, and who finally remembered after a time, said: “I will inform you of its interpretation; send me forth.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God set Joseph apart from his peers by two things: the beauty of his form and the surplus of knowledge; his beauty became the cause of his trial, and his knowledge became the cause of his salvation — that one may know the dignity of knowledge. It is said: “knowledge is given, even if it tarry.”

versets.ar: يُوسُفُ أَيُّهَا الصِّدِّيقُ أَفْتِنَا فِي سَبْعِ بَقَرَاتٍ سِمَانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجَافٌ وَسَبْعِ سُنبُلَاتٍ خُضْرٍ وَأُخَرَ يَابِسَاتٍ لَّعَلِّي أَرْجِعُ إِلَى النَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yūsufu ayyuhā ṣ-ṣiddīqu aftinā fī sabʿi baqarātin simānin yaʾkuluhunna sabʿun ʿijāfun wa-sabʿi sunbulātin khuḍrin wa-ukhara yābisātin laʿallī arjiʿu ilā n-nāsi laʿallahum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: “O Joseph, you who are truthful, tell us what seven fat cows devoured by seven lean ones mean, and seven green ears and others withered. Perhaps I shall return to those who sent me, that they may know.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when God wills a thing, He makes its causes easy — the hearts of the dignitaries were thus seized, the interpretation veiled from them, and the king’s peace was attained only by way of Joseph. True knowledge leads to deliverance.

versets.ar: قَالَ تَزْرَعُونَ سَبْعَ سِنِينَ دَأَبًا فَمَا حَصَدتُّمْ فَذَرُوهُ فِي سُنبُلِهِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ
versets.tr: qāla tazraʿūna sabʿa sinīna daʾaban fa-mā ḥaṣadtum fa-dharūhu fī sunbulihi illā qalīlan mimmā taʾkulūna
versets.trad: He said: “You shall sow for seven years as is your wont. What you reap leave in its ear, save a little of which you eat.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he did not preface the interpretation with the call to tawḥīd this time — for his first interlocutor had forgotten, or he sensed in the king a heart too hard for the call. Wisdom is measured by readiness; sometimes one preaches, sometimes one only counsels.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ يَأْتِي مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ سَبْعٌ شِدَادٌ يَأْكُلْنَ مَا قَدَّمْتُمْ لَهُنَّ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّا تُحْصِنُونَ
versets.tr: thumma yaʾtī min baʿdi dhālika sabʿun shidādun yaʾkulna mā qaddamtum lahunna illā qalīlan mimmā tuḥṣinūna
versets.trad: Then will come, after that, seven hard years that will eat what you have stored for them, save a little that you preserve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī does not dwell specifically on this verse — he sees here Joseph’s wisdom unfolding: after the seven fat come the seven lean. The grace of interpretation foresees need before it arises, and orders human providence by the light of knowledge.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ يَأْتِي مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ عَامٌ فِيهِ يُغَاثُ النَّاسُ وَفِيهِ يَعْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: thumma yaʾtī min baʿdi dhālika ʿāmun fīhi yughāthu n-nāsu wa-fīhi yaʿṣirūna
versets.trad: Then will come, after that, a year in which the people will be given rain abundantly and in which they will press.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph adds, in surplus of knowledge, what the king had not seen in dream — the year of relief. The ṣiddīq sees beyond the question asked. Thus the truthful servant receives, with the answer to the asker, a further opening.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الْمَلِكُ ائْتُونِي بِهِ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُ الرَّسُولُ قَالَ ارْجِعْ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَاسْأَلْهُ مَا بَالُ النِّسْوَةِ اللَّاتِي قَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَّ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّي بِكَيْدِهِنَّ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla l-maliku iʾtūnī bihi fa-lammā jāʾahu r-rasūlu qāla rjiʿ ilā rabbika fa-sʾalhu mā bālu n-niswati llātī qaṭṭaʿna aydiyahunna inna rabbī bi-kaydihinna ʿalīmun
versets.trad: The king said: “Bring him to me!” When the messenger came to him, Joseph said: “Return to your lord and ask him what was the case of the women who cut their hands. My Lord knows their guile perfectly.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph did not want the king to look upon him with the eye of suspicion of betrayal, for the flaw would lower him in his heart and his word would no longer carry — so he waited for his innocence to break forth. The sage knows how to preserve the credit of his word before engaging it in counsel.

versets.ar: قَالَ مَا خَطْبُكُنَّ إِذْ رَاوَدتُّنَّ يُوسُفَ عَن نَّفْسِهِ ۚ قُلْنَ حَاشَ لِلَّهِ مَا عَلِمْنَا عَلَيْهِ مِن سُوءٍ ۚ قَالَتِ امْرَأَتُ الْعَزِيزِ الْآنَ حَصْحَصَ الْحَقُّ أَنَا رَاوَدتُّهُ عَن نَّفْسِهِ وَإِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qāla mā khaṭbukunna idh rāwadttunna yūsufa ʿan nafsihi qulna ḥāsha li-llāhi mā ʿalimnā ʿalayhi min sūʾin qālati mraʾatu l-ʿazīzi l-āna ḥaṣḥaṣa l-ḥaqqu anā rāwadttuhu ʿan nafsihi wa-innahu la-mina ṣ-ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: He said: “What was your business when you sought to seduce Joseph?” They said: “God forfend! We know no evil of him.” The chamberlain’s wife said: “Now the truth has come to light: it was I who sought to seduce him; he is truly of the truthful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: truths never remain hidden in the end — they appear, even if after a while. Joseph was accused while innocent; God turned the matter, made his adversaries speak, and revealed his state. And of the full confession: the perfecting of love brings the tearing of the veil, scant concern for outward or hidden.

mots.racine: ح ص ح ص / ح ق ق

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ لِيَعْلَمَ أَنِّي لَمْ أَخُنْهُ بِالْغَيْبِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي كَيْدَ الْخَائِنِينَ
versets.tr: dhālika li-yaʿlama annī lam akhunhu bi-l-ghaybi wa-anna llāha lā yahdī kayda l-khāʾinīna
versets.trad: That is so he [my husband] may know I did not betray him in his absence, and that God does not guide the guile of the treacherous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God willed to manifest Joseph’s innocence, knowing they deserved punishment for their slander; but Joseph did not wish that, because of him, a chastisement fall upon them — such is the mark of the friends (awliyāʾ): to be the adversaries of their own souls rather than of others.

versets.ar: وَمَا أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِي ۚ إِنَّ النَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ إِلَّا مَا رَحِمَ رَبِّي ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّي غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā ubarriʾu nafsī inna n-nafsa la-ammāratun bi-s-sūʾi illā mā raḥima rabbī inna rabbī ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Yet I do not absolve my own soul: truly the soul is given to commanding evil, save when my Lord shows mercy. Indeed my Lord is Forgiving, Merciful.”
versets.commentaire: Pivotal verse. Qushayrī: when he praised himself with ‘he will know that I did not betray him in his absence,’ it was as if a voice called him in his secret: and not even when you inclined? — so he said: ‘I do not absolve my soul.’ Joseph confesses no preservation comes from him: the first speech shows gratitude for preservation received; the second, confession of impotence — by gratitude he merited the increase of favour, by confession he merited forgiveness.

mots.racine: أ م ر / س و أ

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الْمَلِكُ ائْتُونِي بِهِ أَسْتَخْلِصْهُ لِنَفْسِي ۖ فَلَمَّا كَلَّمَهُ قَالَ إِنَّكَ الْيَوْمَ لَدَيْنَا مَكِينٌ أَمِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla l-maliku iʾtūnī bihi astakhliṣhu li-nafsī fa-lammā kallamahu qāla innaka l-yawma ladaynā makīnun amīnun
versets.trad: The king said: “Bring him to me! I shall attach him to my own person.” When he had spoken to him, the king continued: “Today you are with us established and trusted.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the purity of his act and the nobility of his state became manifest to the king, he summoned him to reserve him to himself; having heard his speech, he raised his rank and his place and guaranteed his generosity. The patient silence in prison reverses into high speech of authority.

mots.sens: I shall attach him to myself

mots.racine: م ك ن / أ م ن

versets.ar: قَالَ اجْعَلْنِي عَلَىٰ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْضِ ۖ إِنِّي حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: qāla jʿalnī ʿalā khazāʾini l-arḍi innī ḥafīẓun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: He said: “Appoint me over the storehouses of the land; truly I am a knowing keeper.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he asked it only to put truth in its place, to bring to the poor their portion — he sought God’s right, not an advantage for himself. And he did not say “I am beautiful,” but “I am a keeper, knowing” — that one may know the merit lies in the inner meaning, not in the form.

mots.racine: خ ز ن / أ ر ض
ح ف ظ / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يَتَبَوَّأُ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ ۚ نُصِيبُ بِرَحْمَتِنَا مَن نَّشَاءُ ۖ وَلَا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika makkannā li-yūsufa fī l-arḍi yatabawwaʾu minhā ḥaythu yashāʾu nuṣību bi-raḥmatinā man nashāʾu wa-lā nuḍīʿu ajra l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: Thus We firmly established Joseph in the land; he could settle wherever he willed. We bestow Our mercy on whom We will, and We do not waste the wage of those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because he had within him no callings of desire, God established him in His kingdom. Then God informs of the pure truth of tawḥīd: “We bestow Our mercy on whom We will” — He fulfils His servants by His grace, not by their act; by His mercy, not by their service.

versets.ar: وَلَأَجْرُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكَانُوا يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-ajru l-ākhirati khayrun lilladhīna āmanū wa-kānū yattaqūna
versets.trad: And the wage of the hereafter is better for those who believe and are godfearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: aspiration must be raised above the visible gift — that one may know there is no salvation without godfearing (taqwā) and resistance to passion. The reminder of the hereafter corrects the brilliance of earthly rule.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / أ خ ر

versets.ar: وَجَاءَ إِخْوَةُ يُوسُفَ فَدَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُ مُنكِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾa ikhwatu yūsufa fa-dakhalū ʿalayhi fa-ʿarafahum wa-hum lahu munkirūna
versets.trad: Joseph’s brothers came and entered upon him; he recognised them, but they did not recognise him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph recognised his brothers and they failed to recognise him, because they believed him still under the condition of slavery — and Joseph at that moment was seated in the king’s place; he who seeks the king under the figure of the slave, when will he know him? Transparent image of the Knower who sees the Known under forms one does not suspect.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُم بِجَهَازِهِمْ قَالَ ائْتُونِي بِأَخٍ لَّكُم مِّنْ أَبِيكُمْ ۚ أَلَا تَرَوْنَ أَنِّي أُوفِي الْكَيْلَ وَأَنَا خَيْرُ الْمُنزِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā jahhazahum bi-jahāzihim qāla iʾtūnī bi-akhin lakum min abīkum a-lā tarawna annī ūfī l-kayla wa-anā khayru l-munzilīna
versets.trad: When he had supplied them with their provisions, he said: “Bring me a brother of yours from your father. Do you not see I give full measure and am the best of hosts?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the lover is jealous: Jacob found consolation in looking upon Benjamin — Joseph was jealous that Jacob looked upon him. And Joseph combined attraction by generosity (“I give full measure”) and threat by deprivation.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ك ي ل

versets.ar: فَإِن لَّمْ تَأْتُونِي بِهِ فَلَا كَيْلَ لَكُمْ عِندِي وَلَا تَقْرَبُونِ
versets.tr: fa-in lam taʾtūnī bihi fa-lā kayla lakum ʿindī wa-lā taqrabūni
versets.trad: If you do not bring him to me, you shall have no measure with me and you shall not come near me.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: measured tactic of the truthful — the threat doubles the attraction, that hearts may turn toward the request. The apparent firmness is here an act of love: it will lead to return and full recognition.

versets.ar: قَالُوا سَنُرَاوِدُ عَنْهُ أَبَاهُ وَإِنَّا لَفَاعِلُونَ
versets.tr: qālū sa-nurāwidu ʿanhu abāhu wa-innā la-fāʿilūna
versets.trad: They said: “We shall plead for him with his father; truly we shall do our best.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph, knowing they had sold him for a paltry price, knew they would come back with their brother out of desire for the full measure — it would not be difficult for them. Sounding of hearts: Joseph gauges their attachment by their interest.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ لِفِتْيَانِهِ اجْعَلُوا بِضَاعَتَهُمْ فِي رِحَالِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْرِفُونَهَا إِذَا انقَلَبُوا إِلَىٰ أَهْلِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla li-fityānihi jʿalū biḍāʿatahum fī riḥālihim laʿallahum yaʿrifūnahā idhā nqalabū ilā ahlihim laʿallahum yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: He said to his young servants: “Put their goods back in their saddlebags, so that they may recognise them when they return to their families; perhaps then they will come back.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: placing their goods in their bags, by the law of generosity, is more perfect than giving them openly — for open giving is a gift with mention, hidden giving is a gift bare of all evocation. It is also the nature of God’s bounties: they arrive in secret — few notice them.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا رَجَعُوا إِلَىٰ أَبِيهِمْ قَالُوا يَا أَبَانَا مُنِعَ مِنَّا الْكَيْلُ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنَا أَخَانَا نَكْتَلْ وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحَافِظُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā rajaʿū ilā abīhim qālū yā abānā muniʿa minnā l-kaylu fa-arsil maʿanā akhānā naktal wa-innā lahu la-ḥāfiẓūna
versets.trad: Once returned to their father, they said: “O our father! The measure has been denied us. So send our brother with us, that we may obtain it; truly we shall watch over him.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph did not deny them the measure — how could he, when he said ‘do you not see I give it in full’? But they exaggerated to shake Jacob’s heart.

versets.ar: قَالَ هَلْ آمَنُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا كَمَا أَمِنتُكُمْ عَلَىٰ أَخِيهِ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ فَاللَّهُ خَيْرٌ حَافِظًا ۖ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla hal āmanukum ʿalayhi illā kamā amintukum ʿalā akhīhi min qablu fa-llāhu khayrun ḥāfiẓan wa-huwa arḥamu r-rāḥimīna
versets.trad: He replied: “Shall I entrust him to you as I entrusted his brother before? But God is the best Keeper; He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who has known betrayal no longer contemplates guarantee — Jacob no longer lets himself be calmed by their promises, aware of their past. He did not say ‘God is better than the one who will bring him back to me,’ and perhaps had he said it, God would have brought him back sooner.

mots.sens: the Most Merciful of the merciful

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا فَتَحُوا مَتَاعَهُمْ وَجَدُوا بِضَاعَتَهُمْ رُدَّتْ إِلَيْهِمْ ۖ قَالُوا يَا أَبَانَا مَا نَبْغِي ۖ هَٰذِهِ بِضَاعَتُنَا رُدَّتْ إِلَيْنَا ۖ وَنَمِيرُ أَهْلَنَا وَنَحْفَظُ أَخَانَا وَنَزْدَادُ كَيْلَ بَعِيرٍ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ كَيْلٌ يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lammā fataḥū matāʿahum wajadū biḍāʿatahum ruddat ilayhim qālū yā abānā mā nabghī hādhihi biḍāʿatunā ruddat ilaynā wa-namīru ahlanā wa-naḥfaẓu akhānā wa-nazdādu kayla baʿīrin dhālika kaylun yasīrun
versets.trad: When they opened their goods they found their merchandise returned to them. They said: “O our father! What more could we desire? Here are our goods returned to us. We shall provision our family, protect our brother, and add a camel’s load: that is an easy measure!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph showed that in his dealing with them he did not need to take payment — when he sold them and gathered their measure, he took no price. Image of “if you do good, you do it for yourselves”: every step the servant takes for religion, God rewards him by joining for him the sweetness of obedience and the savour of living.

versets.ar: قَالَ لَنْ أُرْسِلَهُ مَعَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ تُؤْتُونِ مَوْثِقًا مِّنَ اللَّهِ لَتَأْتُنَّنِي بِهِ إِلَّا أَن يُحَاطَ بِكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا آتَوْهُ مَوْثِقَهُمْ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ
versets.tr: qāla lan ursilahu maʿakum ḥattā tuʾtūni mawthiqan mina llāhi la-taʾtunnanī bihi illā an yuḥāṭa bikum fa-lammā ātawhu mawthiqahum qāla llāhu ʿalā mā naqūlu wakīlun
versets.trad: He said: “I shall not send him with you until you give me, by God, a solemn pledge to bring him back to me, save in case you are overwhelmed.” When they had given him their pledge he said: “God is guarantor of what we say!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: precaution does not protect from decree. Jacob did for Benjamin everything prudence allowed — pacts, sermons, guarantees — and it preserved him not at all from what God had decreed.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ يَا بَنِيَّ لَا تَدْخُلُوا مِن بَابٍ وَاحِدٍ وَادْخُلُوا مِنْ أَبْوَابٍ مُّتَفَرِّقَةٍ ۖ وَمَا أُغْنِي عَنكُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۖ إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۖ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ۖ وَعَلَيْهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla yā baniyya lā tadkhulū min bābin wāḥidin wa-dkhulū min abwābin mutafarriqatin wa-mā ughnī ʿankum mina llāhi min shayʾin ini l-ḥukmu illā li-llāhi ʿalayhi tawakkaltu wa-ʿalayhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-mutawakkilūna
versets.trad: He said also: “O my sons! Do not enter [the city] by one gate, but enter by different gates. I cannot avail you anything against God. The judgment is God’s alone. On Him I rely, and on Him let the reliers rely!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Jacob conjoins cause and tawakkul — he prescribes precaution, then at once confesses: “I cannot avail you anything against God; the judgment is God’s alone”. Perfect model: take the means, surrender the outcome.

mots.racine: ب و ب / ف ر ق

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَهُمْ أَبُوهُم مَّا كَانَ يُغْنِي عَنْهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ مِن شَيْءٍ إِلَّا حَاجَةً فِي نَفْسِ يَعْقُوبَ قَضَاهَا ۚ وَإِنَّهُ لَذُو عِلْمٍ لِّمَا عَلَّمْنَاهُ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā dakhalū min ḥaythu amarahum abūhum mā kāna yughnī ʿanhum mina llāhi min shayʾin illā ḥājatan fī nafsi yaʿqūba qaḍāhā wa-innahu la-dhū ʿilmin li-mā ʿallamnāhu wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: So they entered as their father had ordered them — though it would have availed them nothing against God, except that the matter had, in Jacob’s spirit, the import of a divine decree. For Jacob possessed knowledge of what We had taught him; but most people know not.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: even if Jacob’s purpose did not come about in the end, his desire received its share in the moment — and in this measure, for the men of heart, there is sufficiency. And: one should not believe that all the masters will, comes about — He whose will brooks no denial is God alone.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ آوَىٰ إِلَيْهِ أَخَاهُ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّي أَنَا أَخُوكَ فَلَا تَبْتَئِسْ بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā dakhalū ʿalā yūsufa āwā ilayhi akhāhu qāla innī anā akhūka fa-lā tabtaʾis bi-mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: When they entered upon Joseph he took his (younger) brother aside and said: “I am your brother; do not grieve for what they have done.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Jacob awaited the meeting with Joseph for years; Joseph longed for Benjamin and was granted his sight in the briefest time. Such is love: one is held in suspense, another tried. And: if Jacob’s eye was kindled by separation, Joseph’s eye was refreshed by reunion; the sun does not set on a people but rises on others.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُم بِجَهَازِهِمْ جَعَلَ السِّقَايَةَ فِي رَحْلِ أَخِيهِ ثُمَّ أَذَّنَ مُؤَذِّنٌ أَيَّتُهَا الْعِيرُ إِنَّكُمْ لَسَارِقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jahhazahum bi-jahāzihim jaʿala s-siqāyata fī raḥli akhīhi thumma adhdhana muʾadhdhinun ayyatuhā l-ʿīru innakum la-sāriqūna
versets.trad: Once he had supplied them with their provisions, he placed the cup in his (younger) brother’s pack. Then a herald proclaimed: “O caravan! You are thieves!” Turning back,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Benjamin bore the accusation of theft after finding Joseph. What was imputed to him of evil weighed lightly against what he found of union. Sufism of blame: he bore outwardly the weight of slander, and inwardly he was carried by the finding of nobility.

versets.ar: قَالُوا وَأَقْبَلُوا عَلَيْهِم مَّاذَا تَفْقِدُونَ
versets.tr: qālū wa-aqbalū ʿalayhim mādhā tafqidūna
versets.trad: They said, having turned toward them: “What are you missing?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī passes quickly over this verse — he prepares the confrontation. The brothers advance believing in the purity of their cause; the hidden cup will overturn their assurance.

versets.ar: قَالُوا نَفْقِدُ صُوَاعَ الْمَلِكِ وَلِمَن جَاءَ بِهِ حِمْلُ بَعِيرٍ وَأَنَا بِهِ زَعِيمٌ
versets.tr: qālū nafqidu ṣuwāʿa l-maliki wa-li-man jāʾa bihi ḥimlu baʿīrin wa-anā bihi zaʿīmun
versets.trad: They (the Egyptians) replied: “We are missing the king’s cup; whoever returns it shall have a camel’s load; I stand surety for it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the whole staging is Joseph’s — We suggested this stratagem to Joseph, for he could not, by the royal law, hold back his brother otherwise. The divine ruse runs through the customs of men to serve the love it shelters.

mots.racine: ص و ع / م ل ك

versets.ar: قَالُوا تَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتُم مَّا جِئْنَا لِنُفْسِدَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا كُنَّا سَارِقِينَ
versets.tr: qālū ta-llāhi laqad ʿalimtum mā jiʾnā li-nufsida fī l-arḍi wa-mā kunnā sāriqīna
versets.trad: They (Joseph’s brothers) said: “By God! You know we did not come to spread corruption in the land, and we are no thieves.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the beauty of our outer conduct testifies to the beauty of our inner; were we thieves of your goods, we would not have returned them — you would not have found them in our packs after our departure.

versets.ar: قَالُوا فَمَا جَزَاؤُهُ إِن كُنتُمْ كَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: qālū fa-mā jazāʾuhu in kuntum kādhibīna
versets.trad: They (the Egyptians) returned: “What then shall be his requital, if you are lying?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they turn the question back so that the brothers themselves pronounce the sentence — art of the providential ruse: the cause closes upon those who pronounce it.

versets.ar: قَالُوا جَزَاؤُهُ مَن وُجِدَ فِي رَحْلِهِ فَهُوَ جَزَاؤُهُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: qālū jazāʾuhu man wujida fī raḥlihi fa-huwa jazāʾuhu kadhālika najzī ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: They (the brothers) replied: “His punishment? The one in whose pack the cup is found shall be delivered to you in atonement. Thus we punish the unjust!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the brothers had the boldness to pronounce the sentence themselves, confident in their innocence — Benjamin being their companion in this apparent innocence. But they did not know the divine stratagem that would turn their word into a snare.

versets.ar: فَبَدَأَ بِأَوْعِيَتِهِمْ قَبْلَ وِعَاءِ أَخِيهِ ثُمَّ اسْتَخْرَجَهَا مِن وِعَاءِ أَخِيهِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كِدْنَا لِيُوسُفَ ۖ مَا كَانَ لِيَأْخُذَ أَخَاهُ فِي دِينِ الْمَلِكِ إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ ۚ نَرْفَعُ دَرَجَاتٍ مَّن نَّشَاءُ ۗ وَفَوْقَ كُلِّ ذِي عِلْمٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-badaʾa bi-awʿiyatihim qabla wiʿāʾi akhīhi thumma stakhrajahā min wiʿāʾi akhīhi kadhālika kidnā li-yūsufa mā kāna li-yaʾkhudha akhāhu fī dīni l-maliki illā an yashāʾa llāhu narfaʿu darajātin man nashāʾu wa-fawqa kulli dhī ʿilmin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Joseph began with the bags of the other brothers; then he searched his (younger) brother’s bag and brought out the cup from it. Thus did We suggest a stratagem to Joseph, for he could not, by the king’s law, take his brother had God not so willed. We raise in rank whom We will. Above every knower is the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the brothers raised their voice in blame; as for Benjamin, he kept silent — as if confessing the theft, which was not — but had he denied, he would have aired Joseph’s secret. His tongue fell silent, and his heart bloomed in the true state. Silent model of the man of blame (malāmatī).

versets.ar: قَالُوا إِن يَسْرِقْ فَقَدْ سَرَقَ أَخٌ لَّهُ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ فَأَسَرَّهَا يُوسُفُ فِي نَفْسِهِ وَلَمْ يُبْدِهَا لَهُمْ ۚ قَالَ أَنتُمْ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا ۖ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَصِفُونَ
versets.tr: qālū in yasriq fa-qad saraqa akhun lahu min qablu fa-asarrahā yūsufu fī nafsihi wa-lam yubdihā lahum qāla antum sharrun makānan wa-llāhu aʿlamu bi-mā taṣifūna
versets.trad: They (the brothers) said: “If he has stolen, a brother of his stole before.” Joseph kept the secret of what had passed and revealed nothing to them. He said: “You are in a vile position! God knows perfectly what you insinuate.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: wounding by slander was the most painful thing Joseph heard from them: ‘if he has stolen, a brother of his stole before’ — a harder blow in his heart than the first injury. Law of the great: even when the king silences his anger, do not feel safe; he will end by making it felt, even after long.

mots.racine: ش ر ر / ك و ن

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ إِنَّ لَهُ أَبًا شَيْخًا كَبِيرًا فَخُذْ أَحَدَنَا مَكَانَهُ ۖ إِنَّا نَرَاكَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: qālū yā ayyuhā l-ʿazīzu inna lahu aban shaykhan kabīran fa-khudh aḥadanā makānahu innā narāka mina l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: They said: “O Excellence! He has an aged father; take one of us in his place. We see you among those who do good.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: no soul bears the burden of another — the father is not taken in the son’s place, nor the relative accepted in another’s stead. Each is held by his own act.

mots.racine: ش ي خ / ك ب ر

versets.ar: قَالَ مَعَاذَ اللَّهِ أَن نَّأْخُذَ إِلَّا مَن وَجَدْنَا مَتَاعَنَا عِندَهُ إِنَّا إِذًا لَّظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla maʿādha llāhi an naʾkhudha illā man wajadnā matāʿanā ʿindahu innā idhan la-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: He said: “God forbid that we should take any but him in whose possession we found our property! To do so would be injustice.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they imagined it was a money affair — they did not know that Joseph was scheming with them out of love for his brother, and that the beloved never suffers a substitute.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا اسْتَيْأَسُوا مِنْهُ خَلَصُوا نَجِيًّا ۖ قَالَ كَبِيرُهُمْ أَلَمْ تَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ أَبَاكُمْ قَدْ أَخَذَ عَلَيْكُم مَّوْثِقًا مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَمِن قَبْلُ مَا فَرَّطتُمْ فِي يُوسُفَ ۖ فَلَنْ أَبْرَحَ الْأَرْضَ حَتَّىٰ يَأْذَنَ لِي أَبِي أَوْ يَحْكُمَ اللَّهُ لِي ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā stayʾasū minhu khalaṣū najiyyan qāla kabīruhum a-lam taʿlamū anna abākum qad akhadha ʿalaykum mawthiqan mina llāhi wa-min qablu mā farraṭtum fī yūsufa fa-lan abraḥa l-arḍa ḥattā yaʾdhana lī abī aw yaḥkuma llāhu lī wa-huwa khayru l-ḥākimīna
versets.trad: Despairing of moving him, they withdrew to confer. The eldest said: “Do you not know that your father has taken from you a solemn pledge before God, and that already in the past you failed concerning Joseph? I shall not leave this land until my father gives me leave or God decrees for me. For He is the best of judges.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they saw Joseph would not part from his brother, they withdrew aside; shame seized them, they knew Jacob would see in them once more the same matter as before. The similarity of trials reveals the rigour of the inner reckoning.

versets.ar: ارْجِعُوا إِلَىٰ أَبِيكُمْ فَقُولُوا يَا أَبَانَا إِنَّ ابْنَكَ سَرَقَ وَمَا شَهِدْنَا إِلَّا بِمَا عَلِمْنَا وَمَا كُنَّا لِلْغَيْبِ حَافِظِينَ
versets.tr: irjiʿū ilā abīkum fa-qūlū yā abānā inna bnaka saraqa wa-mā shahidnā illā bi-mā ʿalimnā wa-mā kunnā li-l-ghaybi ḥāfiẓīna
versets.trad: Return to your father and tell him: “O our father! Your son has indeed stolen; we report only what we know, without being able to pronounce on what is hidden.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this time they have visible proof, but Jacob’s heart finds no peace — for the first fault made the suspicion permanent. He who lied once will no longer be believed, even when he speaks the truth.

mots.racine: غ ي ب / ح ف ظ

versets.ar: وَاسْأَلِ الْقَرْيَةَ الَّتِي كُنَّا فِيهَا وَالْعِيرَ الَّتِي أَقْبَلْنَا فِيهَا ۖ وَإِنَّا لَصَادِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-sʾali l-qaryata llatī kunnā fīhā wa-l-ʿīra llatī aqbalnā fīhā wa-innā la-ṣādiqūna
versets.trad: Ask the people of the city where we were and the caravan with which we came; truly we speak the truth!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the more they multiply proofs, the more Jacob finds ground for doubt. And: questioning vestiges is a custom of loving hearts — there lies consolation for their secrets.

versets.ar: قَالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ لَكُمْ أَنفُسُكُمْ أَمْرًا ۖ فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ۖ عَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَأْتِيَنِي بِهِمْ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: qāla bal sawwalat lakum anfusukum amran fa-ṣabrun jamīlun ʿasā llāhu an yaʾtiyanī bihim jamīʿan innahu huwa l-ʿalīmu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Jacob said: “Nay! Your souls have prompted you to some scheme. Beautiful patience! Perhaps God will bring them all back to me. He is truly the All-Knowing, the Wise!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he took refuge in the nearness of deliverance through patience. And: he had pledged himself to patience — but he did not wait till evening to say ‘alas for Joseph’; that one may know the lovers’ resolve to be patient is ever broken.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ج م ل

versets.ar: وَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَا أَسَفَىٰ عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ وَابْيَضَّتْ عَيْنَاهُ مِنَ الْحُزْنِ فَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-tawallā ʿanhum wa-qāla yā asafā ʿalā yūsufa wa-byaḍḍat ʿaynāhu mina l-ḥuzni fa-huwa kaẓīmun
versets.trad: And he turned away from them and said: “Alas, poor Joseph!” And his two eyes whitened from the grief he was struggling to contain.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports al-Daqqāq: “God did not say ‘Jacob became blind’ but ‘his two eyes whitened’: for it was not in truth blindness, but a veil from seeing other than Joseph”. And: the absence of sight was a gift to him, that he should no longer need to look upon what was not the beloved — nothing is harder for lovers than to see other than their beloved in his absence.

versets.ar: قَالُوا تَاللَّهِ تَفْتَأُ تَذْكُرُ يُوسُفَ حَتَّىٰ تَكُونَ حَرَضًا أَوْ تَكُونَ مِنَ الْهَالِكِينَ
versets.tr: qālū ta-llāhi taftaʾu tadhkuru yūsufa ḥattā takūna ḥaraḍan aw takūna mina l-hālikīna
versets.trad: They said: “By God! You will not cease remembering Joseph until you waste away or die of it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sweetest thing in death is what comes from following passion (love) — how can one threaten with death the one to whom death is most beloved? Strange sweetness: the lover does not dread what others dread.

mots.def: ḥaraḍ: at the verge of death.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّمَا أَشْكُو بَثِّي وَحُزْنِي إِلَى اللَّهِ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla innamā ashkū baththī wa-ḥuznī ilā llāhi wa-aʿlamu mina llāhi mā lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: He said: “I complain only to God of my grief and my affliction, and I know, coming from God, what you do not know.
versets.commentaire: Central verse of Jacob’s ethics. Qushayrī: he complained to God and not of God — he who complains to God is joined; he who complains of God is severed. And: bearing in himself the weight of the body, his heart was lightened by the secret and the spirit; he knew from God the truth of his state.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِيَّ اذْهَبُوا فَتَحَسَّسُوا مِن يُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ وَلَا تَيْأَسُوا مِن رَّوْحِ اللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُ لَا يَيْأَسُ مِن رَّوْحِ اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: yā baniyya dhhabū fa-taḥassasū min yūsufa wa-akhīhi wa-lā tayʾasū min rawḥi llāhi innahu lā yayʾasu min rawḥi llāhi illā l-qawmu l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: O my sons! Go and seek out Joseph and his brother. Do not despair of God’s breath (rawḥ), for only the disbelieving people despair of God’s breath.”
themes: do not despair of God’s breath (rawḥ)
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the speech ‘seek out Joseph and his brother’ orders to seek him with all the senses: by sight, that perhaps their eyes may fall on him; by hearing, that they may hear his name; by smell, that they may find his fragrance. And he sends them back to divine grace — only the disbeliever despairs of God’s breath (rawḥ).

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ قَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنَا وَأَهْلَنَا الضُّرُّ وَجِئْنَا بِبِضَاعَةٍ مُّزْجَاةٍ فَأَوْفِ لَنَا الْكَيْلَ وَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَيْنَا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَجْزِي الْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā dakhalū ʿalayhi qālū yā ayyuhā l-ʿazīzu massanā wa-ahlanā ḍ-ḍurru wa-jiʾnā bi-biḍāʿatin muzjātin fa-awfi lanā l-kayla wa-taṣaddaq ʿalaynā inna llāha yajzī l-mutaṣaddiqīna
versets.trad: Once entered upon him they said: “O Excellence! Misfortune has touched us and our family, and we bring only merchandise of slight value. Give us full measure and bestow alms upon us. God truly rewards those who give alms.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they entered upon Joseph they addressed him by mentioning misfortune, saying not a word of Joseph or the cause for which their father had sent them. Then: they descended to the lowest rank, saying ‘bestow alms upon us’ — as if to say: if we do not deserve the dealing of commerce, we deserve the free gift.

mots.racine: ب ض ع / ز ج و

versets.ar: قَالَ هَلْ عَلِمْتُم مَّا فَعَلْتُم بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ إِذْ أَنتُمْ جَاهِلُونَ
versets.tr: qāla hal ʿalimtum mā faʿaltum bi-yūsufa wa-akhīhi idh antum jāhilūna
versets.trad: He said: “Do you know what, in your ignorance, you did to Joseph and his brother?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph said to them: you finished your speech, multiplied your discourse, and there was in your talk only the mention of your distress — did the memory of your brother Joseph not occur to you? This, in matters of reproach, is greater than any chastisement.

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَإِنَّكَ لَأَنتَ يُوسُفُ ۖ قَالَ أَنَا يُوسُفُ وَهَٰذَا أَخِي ۖ قَدْ مَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا ۖ إِنَّهُ مَن يَتَّقِ وَيَصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: qālū a-innaka la-anta yūsufu qāla anā yūsufu wa-hādhā akhī qad manna llāhu ʿalaynā innahu man yattaqi wa-yaṣbir fa-inna llāha lā yuḍīʿu ajra l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: They said: “Is it that you are Joseph?” He answered: “I am Joseph, and this is my brother. God has been gracious to us. Whoever fears God and patiently endures — truly God does not waste the wage of those who do good.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he said ‘I am Joseph and this is my brother,’ and did not say ‘and you are my brothers’ — allusion to a corner of reproach: what you did with me is not the act of brothers. And he at once softened the shock by turning the speech to Benjamin. The verse seals: godfearing and patience bear the divine wage.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ص ب ر

versets.ar: قَالُوا تَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ آثَرَكَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَإِن كُنَّا لَخَاطِئِينَ
versets.tr: qālū ta-llāhi laqad ātharaka llāhu ʿalaynā wa-in kunnā la-khāṭiʾīna
versets.trad: They said: “By God! Indeed God has preferred you over us, and we have been sinners.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports al-Daqqāq: when Joseph said ‘whoever fears and patiently endures,’ he attributed the merit to himself by patience — so God made his brothers speak in the name of tawḥīd: ‘by God! God has preferred you over us’ — meaning: it is neither by your patience nor by your fear, but by God’s election. Then Joseph answered, assenting: “no reproach today.”

versets.ar: قَالَ لَا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ ۖ يَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla lā tathrība ʿalaykumu l-yawma yaghfiru llāhu lakum wa-huwa arḥamu r-rāḥimīna
versets.trad: He said: “No reproach upon you today! May God forgive you! He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Joseph hastened to forgive; Jacob, however, promised them only future intercession (‘I shall ask forgiveness for you’), because he loved them more, and it is intense love that reproaches. Joseph did not deem them worthy of blame, and wiped the slate at the first instant.

versets.ar: اذْهَبُوا بِقَمِيصِي هَٰذَا فَأَلْقُوهُ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِ أَبِي يَأْتِ بَصِيرًا وَأْتُونِي بِأَهْلِكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: idhhabū bi-qamīṣī hādhā fa-alqūhu ʿalā wajhi abī yaʾti baṣīran wa-ʾtūnī bi-ahlikum ajmaʿīna
versets.trad: Take this tunic of mine and lay it upon my father’s face. He will recover his sight; then bring me your family all together.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial, when it comes, comes at once; when it withdraws, it withdraws by degrees: first the fragrance of Joseph, then his tunic, then the arrival before him, then his sight. And: the tunic serves only to be worn — save the lovers’ tunic: it serves only to find the fragrance of the beloveds.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا فَصَلَتِ الْعِيرُ قَالَ أَبُوهُمْ إِنِّي لَأَجِدُ رِيحَ يُوسُفَ ۖ لَوْلَا أَن تُفَنِّدُونِ
versets.tr: wa-lammā faṣalati l-ʿīru qāla abūhum innī la-ajidu rīḥa yūsufa law-lā an tufannidūni
versets.trad: When the caravan set out, their father said: “I sense the scent of Joseph, even though you think I dote!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Jacob alone found Joseph’s fragrance, because he alone bore the grief of losing Joseph; one knows the fragrance of the beloveds only by love; for strangers this is an obscure word — whence should a man have a fragrance? Law of spiritual discernment: one smells only what one loves.

mots.racine: ف ص ل / ع ي ر

versets.ar: قَالُوا تَاللَّهِ إِنَّكَ لَفِي ضَلَالِكَ الْقَدِيمِ
versets.tr: qālū ta-llāhi innaka la-fī ḍalālika l-qadīmi
versets.trad: They said: “By God! You are in your old error indeed!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they joined insult to their speech, did not respect their father, did not preserve his right in address, and qualified him as ‘astray in love’. Mockery of the blind who do not see the hidden meaning.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ق د م

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا أَن جَاءَ الْبَشِيرُ أَلْقَاهُ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهِ فَارْتَدَّ بَصِيرًا ۖ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā an jāʾa l-bashīru alqāhu ʿalā wajhihi fa-rtadda baṣīran qāla a-lam aqul lakum innī aʿlamu mina llāhi mā lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: When the bearer of good news arrived, he laid the tunic upon Jacob’s face and he recovered his sight. He said: “Did I not tell you that I know, coming from God, what you do not know?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: were Joseph’s tunic cast upon the face of all the blind on earth, their sight would not return; only Jacob’s sight returned with Joseph’s tunic — for his sight had gone by the separation, and by it it came back.

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا أَبَانَا اسْتَغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا إِنَّا كُنَّا خَاطِئِينَ
versets.tr: qālū yā abānā staghfir lanā dhunūbanā innā kunnā khāṭiʾīna
versets.trad: They said: “O our father! Ask forgiveness for us for our sins. Truly we have been sinners!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: each according to his state — Jacob and Joseph in joy and good news; the brothers in embarrassment, seeking excuses and asking pardon. And: day for day: the day Jacob was afflicted by Joseph’s absence, the day he was filled by Joseph’s tunic — and the brothers, in the embarrassment of what they had done.

versets.ar: قَالَ سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّي ۖ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: qāla sawfa astaghfiru lakum rabbī innahu huwa l-ghafūru r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: He said: “I shall ask my Lord forgiveness for you. Truly He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he promised them intercession, because he had not finished his joy to enter into intercession. And: he did not answer them on the spot, to let them feel the evil they had committed — Joseph being absent at that moment, while the greater portion of the right belonged to Joseph.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ آوَىٰ إِلَيْهِ أَبَوَيْهِ وَقَالَ ادْخُلُوا مِصْرَ إِن شَاءَ اللَّهُ آمِنِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā dakhalū ʿalā yūsufa āwā ilayhi abawayhi wa-qāla dkhulū miṣra in shāʾa llāhu āminīna
versets.trad: When they (Jacob and his family) arrived to Joseph, he received his father and his mother and said: “Enter Egypt in safety, if God wills!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they all entered, but they parted in the welcome: Joseph singled out his parents by embrace, because they were far from harshness. Image of the Day: all enter the Garden, but the ranks of nearness set apart the pure.

versets.ar: وَرَفَعَ أَبَوَيْهِ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ وَخَرُّوا لَهُ سُجَّدًا ۖ وَقَالَ يَا أَبَتِ هَٰذَا تَأْوِيلُ رُؤْيَايَ مِن قَبْلُ قَدْ جَعَلَهَا رَبِّي حَقًّا ۖ وَقَدْ أَحْسَنَ بِي إِذْ أَخْرَجَنِي مِنَ السِّجْنِ وَجَاءَ بِكُم مِّنَ الْبَدْوِ مِن بَعْدِ أَن نَّزَغَ الشَّيْطَانُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ إِخْوَتِي ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّي لَطِيفٌ لِّمَا يَشَاءُ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-rafaʿa abawayhi ʿalā l-ʿarshi wa-kharrū lahu sujjadan wa-qāla yā abati hādhā taʾwīlu ruʾyāya min qablu qad jaʿalahā rabbī ḥaqqan wa-qad aḥsana bī idh akhrajanī mina s-sijni wa-jāʾa bikum mina l-badwi min baʿdi an nazagha sh-shayṭānu baynī wa-bayna ikhwatī inna rabbī laṭīfun li-mā yashāʾu innahu huwa l-ʿalīmu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: He raised his father and his mother upon the throne and they prostrated before him. He said: “O my father! This is the realisation of my former vision: my Lord has made it true. He has been kind to me when He brought me out of prison and brought you from the desert after the Devil had stirred up discord between me and my brothers. Truly my Lord is gentle toward what He wills; He is the All-Knowing, the Wise!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he mentioned the prison rather than the well — because the trial there lasted long and the well’s was brief; because to evoke the well would have shamed the brothers; because in the well he was accompanied (the beginner is treated gently) while in prison this gentleness vanishes (the strong is set to a sterner trial). And: “the devil stirred up discord between me and my brothers” — a way of sharing the lack, an elegant veil over their fault.

versets.ar: رَبِّ قَدْ آتَيْتَنِي مِنَ الْمُلْكِ وَعَلَّمْتَنِي مِن تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ ۚ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَنتَ وَلِيِّي فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۖ تَوَفَّنِي مُسْلِمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: rabbi qad ātaytanī mina l-mulki wa-ʿallamtanī min taʾwīli l-aḥādīthi fāṭira s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi anta waliyyī fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati tawaffanī musliman wa-alḥiqnī bi-ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: O my Lord! You have given me sovereignty and taught me the interpretation of events. Originator of the heavens and the earth, You are my Protector in this world and in the next. Make me die in submission (muslim) and place me among the righteous!”
