versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports al-Daqqāq: the signs of yearning (ishtiyāq) are to wish for death upon the carpet of wellbeing — Joseph was cast into the well without saying ‘make me die a muslim’; placed for sale, he did not say it; imprisoned for years, he did not say it; and when the kingship was given to him, when he met his brothers prostrate and his parents upon the throne, then he said ‘make me die a muslim’ — proof that he aspired to the divine encounter.

mots.racine: ف ط ر / س م و
و ف ي / س ل م

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنبَاءِ الْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهِ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَمَا كُنتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ أَجْمَعُوا أَمْرَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَمْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika min anbāʾi l-ghaybi nūḥīhi ilayka wa-mā kunta ladayhim idh ajmaʿū amrahum wa-hum yamkurūna
versets.trad: These are tidings of the unseen which We reveal to you. You were not with them when they agreed upon their affair and were scheming.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it becomes evident that such an exposition could only have come to an illiterate man by celestial teaching. The Prophet’s being unlettered at the beginning is the mark of his nobility at the end — it is precisely because he is ummī that his truthfulness is recognised.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / غ ي ب

versets.ar: وَمَا أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ وَلَوْ حَرَصْتَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā aktharu n-nāsi wa-law ḥaraṣta bi-muʾminīna
versets.trad: Yet most people, even with your eagerness, will not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has established you as witness of His will to awaken faith in them and of the intensity of the longing to see it accomplished in them; then I know that most will not believe, and I let you know it — and impose upon you at the same time to aspire to what I know will not be.

versets.ar: وَمَا تَسْأَلُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā tasʾaluhum ʿalayhi min ajrin in huwa illā dhikrun li-l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: You ask of them no recompense in return. It is but a reminder for the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: such is God’s way with His prophets: He forbade them to take contrepart or wage for the transmission of the message; and even the scholars — heirs of the prophets — must take nothing from people for the call to God. Whoever takes among them a share will not be blessed in what he has caused to be heard.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَكَأَيِّن مِّنْ آيَةٍ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهَا وَهُمْ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kaʾayyin min āyatin fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi yamurrūna ʿalayhā wa-hum ʿanhā muʿriḍūna
versets.trad: How many signs in the heavens and the earth do they pass by while turning away from them!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the signs are visible, the proofs blazing, and each particle of creatures attests that He is One; but as one who shuts his eye does not enjoy the day’s light, so one who neglects his regard and reflection does not profit from His knowledge and clairvoyance.

versets.ar: وَمَا يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُم بِاللَّهِ إِلَّا وَهُم مُّشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā yuʾminu aktharuhum bi-llāhi illā wa-hum mushrikūna
versets.trad: And most of them do not believe in God without being associators.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes several degrees: manifest association is to take an adored beside God; hidden association is to take, beside Him, an object sought of the heart in need. The Knowers’ association is to look upon a beheld other than Him, or to see other than Him as existent in truth. Fine spectrum of inner shirk.

versets.ar: أَفَأَمِنُوا أَن تَأْتِيَهُمْ غَاشِيَةٌ مِّنْ عَذَابِ اللَّهِ أَوْ تَأْتِيَهُمُ السَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-aminū an taʾtiyahum ghāshiyatun min ʿadhābi llāhi aw taʾtiyahumu s-sāʿatu baghtatan wa-hum lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: Are they then secure that an enveloping punishment from God will not come upon them, or that the Hour will not come upon them suddenly while they do not perceive?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: is he secure who was deceived by the length of the respite, that he will not be struck with extermination? Is he secure who was deceived by the length of wellbeing, that the trial will not stand against him on the Day of Resurrection?

versets.ar: قُلْ هَٰذِهِ سَبِيلِي أَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ ۚ عَلَىٰ بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِي ۖ وَسُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَمَا أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: qul hādhihi sabīlī adʿū ilā llāhi ʿalā baṣīratin anā wa-mani ttabaʿanī wa-subḥāna llāhi wa-mā anā mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Say: “This is my path! I call to God upon clear sight, I and whoever follows me. Glory to God! I am not among the associators.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: baṣīra is the certainty in which is no doubt, the evidence without shadow; its bearer is gently aided in the outward, and entrusted with the hidden of disclosures. And: baṣīra is that the suns of gnosis rise and absorb in them the lights of the stars of reason. Beautiful figure: direct knowledge absorbs reason’s light as the sun swallows the stars.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجَالًا نُّوحِي إِلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرَىٰ ۗ أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۗ وَلَدَارُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā min qablika illā rijālan nūḥī ilayhim min ahli l-qurā a-fa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna min qablihim wa-la-dāru l-ākhirati khayrun lilladhīna ttaqaw a-fa-lā taʿqilūna
versets.trad: And We sent before you none but men, of the people of the towns, to whom We revealed. Have they not journeyed in the land and seen what was the end of those before them? Truly the abode of the hereafter is better for those who are godfearing. Will you not understand?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they marvelled that God would send creatures a human messenger; He explains that such is His custom among past communities — a human messenger. Either they denied the very mission, or they held it strange that a human be sent. And He invites them to reasoning and consideration.

versets.ar: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا اسْتَيْأَسَ الرُّسُلُ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ كُذِبُوا جَاءَهُمْ نَصْرُنَا فَنُجِّيَ مَن نَّشَاءُ ۖ وَلَا يُرَدُّ بَأْسُنَا عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: ḥattā idhā stayʾasa r-rusulu wa-ẓannū annahum qad kudhibū jāʾahum naṣrunā fa-nujjiya man nashāʾu wa-lā yuraddu baʾsunā ʿani l-qawmi l-mujrimīna
versets.trad: Until, when the messengers despaired and thought they had been called liars, Our help came to them. We save whom We will, and Our might is not turned away from the criminal people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has decreed that He opens no states to disciples (murīdīn) except after their despair of them. As He says: “He sends down rain after they have despaired” — just as He sends down rain after despair, He opens the states after despair of them and contentment with being deprived. Deep law of the path: it is in dispossession that the true gift is received.

versets.ar: لَقَدْ كَانَ فِي قَصَصِهِمْ عِبْرَةٌ لِّأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ ۗ مَا كَانَ حَدِيثًا يُفْتَرَىٰ وَلَٰكِن تَصْدِيقَ الَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَتَفْصِيلَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: laqad kāna fī qaṣaṣihim ʿibratun li-ulī l-albābi mā kāna ḥadīthan yuftarā wa-lākin taṣdīqa lladhī bayna yadayhi wa-tafṣīla kulli shayʾin wa-hudan wa-raḥmatan li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Indeed, in their stories is a lesson for those of understanding. It is no fabricated tale, but a confirmation of what was before it, a clarification of all things, a guidance and a mercy for a people who believe.
themes: the lesson for hearts of understanding
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī concludes in fan: a lesson for kings — in the breadth of justice, on the model of Joseph who bestowed upon the Egyptians and freed them when he had come to possess them; a lesson for the men of godfearing — Joseph, having left his passion, was elevated by God; a lesson for the men of passion — the chamberlain’s wife, by following her inclination, met affliction and humiliation; a lesson for servants in the presence of their masters — Joseph, having preserved Zulaykhā’s honour, came at last to possess the chamberlain’s realm. Seal: truthful account, confirmation, detail, guidance, mercy.

ه د ي / ر ح م
